title: Angular Basic — Paper 3
description: Foundational Angular concepts: components, data binding, directives, component communication, lifecycle hooks, services, modules, template-driven forms, pipes, and CLI.

questions.text: Which ViewEncapsulation mode uses the browser's native Shadow DOM to scope styles?
In Angular, which type of data binding reads data from the component class and displays it in the template without allowing the template to update the class?
In Angular 17+, which new control flow block replaces the *ngFor directive?
When using @ViewChild to access a child component, when is the reference first available?
What is the role of the ngDoCheck lifecycle hook?

options.text: It checks if an @Input() has changed
It runs after the view is fully initialized
It allows custom change detection logic beyond Angular's default detection
It destroys the component on the next change detection cycle

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct way to inject a service into a component using the constructor?
What is the 'bootstrap' property in @NgModule used for?

options.text: To specify the root component that Angular inserts into index.html at startup
To list modules that should be eagerly loaded
To declare the main service for the application

questions.text: Which built-in validator attribute in a template-driven form ensures an input field is not left empty?
Which Angular pipe converts an object or array to a JSON string representation for debugging in templates?
Which Angular CLI command runs the unit tests for an application?
When using the inline template option in @Component, which property is used instead of templateUrl?
What is the difference between property binding [src]="imgUrl" and attribute binding [attr.colspan]="colNum"?

options.text: Property binding sets a DOM property; attribute binding sets an HTML attribute that has no DOM property equivalent
There is no difference; both do the same thing
Attribute binding is faster than property binding
Property binding only works on custom components

questions.text: In Angular 17+, what does the @empty block do in the new @for control flow syntax?

options.text: It clears the list before rendering
It provides a fallback template to show when the iterable collection is empty
It renders an empty placeholder while data loads
It hides the element when no items exist

questions.text: Which Angular feature allows a component to project multiple different pieces of content into named slots?

options.text: Multiple ng-content elements with the select attribute

questions.text: What does the ngAfterViewInit lifecycle hook signal about the component's state?

options.text: The component's view (and child views) have been fully initialized
The component's projected content has been initialized

questions.text: What is a singleton service in Angular?

options.text: A service that can only be used in one component
A service where only one instance is created and shared across the application
A service with only one method
A service that creates a new instance every time it is injected

questions.text: What is the difference between a standalone component and a module-based component in Angular?

options.text: Standalone components cannot use pipes or directives
Module-based components use standalone: true in the decorator
A standalone component manages its own dependencies via its imports array and does not need to be declared in an NgModule

questions.text: Which template-driven form state is true when none of the form fields have been changed yet?
What does the 'number' pipe do in Angular?

options.text: Converts a string to a number
Formats a numeric value with locale-specific grouping separators and decimal digits
Rounds a number to the nearest integer
Converts a number to a percentage

questions.text: What does the ng generate service command create?

options.text: A TypeScript class decorated with @Injectable and a corresponding spec file
A new component with a service injected

questions.text: In a standalone Angular component, how is ViewEncapsulation.Emulated different from ViewEncapsulation.ShadowDom?

options.text: Emulated uses real Shadow DOM while ShadowDom uses attribute-based scoping
Emulated simulates scoping by adding unique attributes to elements and selectors; ShadowDom uses the browser's native Shadow DOM API
ShadowDom removes all scoping while Emulated keeps global styles

questions.text: Which Angular binding type responds to user DOM events such as clicks and keystrokes?
In the new Angular control flow syntax (@if, @for, @switch), what is a key advantage over the structural directive approach?

options.text: They require no imports and provide better type narrowing and performance
They work only with standalone components
They are only available in Angular module-based apps

questions.text: How does the child component send data back to the parent component in Angular?

options.text: By modifying the parent component's @Input() property directly
By emitting events through an @Output() EventEmitter property
By setting a shared global variable
By calling a method on the parent via @ViewChild

questions.text: Is it possible for a component to implement multiple lifecycle hook interfaces? For example, implementing both OnInit and OnDestroy?

options.text: No, a component can only implement one lifecycle hook interface
Yes, a component can implement multiple lifecycle hook interfaces
Only two lifecycle interfaces can be implemented at once
Lifecycle hooks are applied automatically and cannot be explicitly implemented

questions.text: When multiple components inject the same service provided in 'root', how many instances of that service are created?

options.text: Two instances: one for the root and one for child components
Only one instance shared across all components

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'exports' property in @NgModule?

options.text: It lists services exported from the module
It lists components, directives, or pipes that other modules can use after importing this module
It exports the module as an ES module
It lists the modules imported by this module

questions.text: In a template-driven form, which event binding is used to handle form submission?
Which pipe extracts a subset of a string or array in Angular?
What does the ng generate module command create in an Angular project?

options.text: A new TypeScript file decorated with @NgModule
A new service with its own module
